question: What is the correct logical SQL execution order from the notes?

options.text: FROM -> JOIN -> WHERE -> GROUP BY -> SELECT -> HAVING -> ORDER BY
options.feedback: HAVING filters grouped results before final SELECT projection.

options.text: FROM -> JOIN -> GROUP BY -> WHERE -> HAVING -> SELECT -> ORDER BY
options.feedback: WHERE filters rows before grouping, not after it.

options.text: FROM -> JOIN -> WHERE -> GROUP BY -> HAVING -> SELECT -> ORDER BY
options.feedback: Correct: source rows are built and filtered before projection and sorting.

options.text: FROM -> JOIN -> WHERE -> SELECT -> GROUP BY -> HAVING -> ORDER BY
options.feedback: SELECT cannot precede grouping because GROUP BY/HAVING shape projection input.

whyQuestion: Why must SQL process FROM and JOIN before SELECT?

whyOptions.text: ORDER BY determines which joined rows survive earlier relational operations.
whyOptions.feedback: ORDER BY sorts final results; it does not decide row eligibility.

whyOptions.text: SELECT must run first so WHERE and GROUP BY can reference select aliases.
whyOptions.feedback: Aliases are not a reason for execution order and often unavailable there.

whyOptions.text: SQL must build the working row set before selecting projected output columns.
whyOptions.feedback: Exactly: projection requires a resolved and filtered relational dataset first.

whyOptions.text: HAVING creates join relationships that FROM and JOIN evaluate afterward.
whyOptions.feedback: HAVING filters existing groups; it does not define joins.

hint: Execution starts with source rows, not projection.
